is a [historic district](/wiki/Historic_district_(United_States) "Historic district (United States)") in the central [business district](/wiki/Business_district "Business district") of [Woonsocket, Rhode Island](/wiki/Woonsocket,_Rhode_Island "Woonsocket, Rhode Island"), USA. It extends along Main Street, between the railroad tracks just northeast of its junction with Clinton Street, and roughly Ascension Street at the southwest. Most of the sixteen buildings in this area were built at the height of Woonsocket's prosperity, roughly between 1850 and 1930. The district is characterized by brick and masonry buildings generally between two and six stories in height.[[2]](#cite_note-NRHP-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Historic district in Rhode Island, United States

United States historic place

| Added to NRHP | April 18, 1991 |

The **Main Street Historic District** is a [historic district](/wiki/Historic_district_(United_States) "Historic district (United States)") in the central [business district](/wiki/Business_district "Business district") of [Woonsocket, Rhode Island](/wiki/Woonsocket,_Rhode_Island "Woonsocket, Rhode Island"), USA. It extends along Main Street, between the railroad tracks just northeast of its junction with Clinton Street, and roughly Ascension Street at the southwest. Most of the sixteen buildings in this area were built at the height of Woonsocket's prosperity, roughly between 1850 and 1930. The district is characterized by brick and masonry buildings generally between two and six stories in height.[[2]](#cite_note-NRHP-2)

The district was listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in 1991.[[1]](#cite_note-nris-1) It includes two properties previously listed on the National Register: [Woonsocket City Hall](/wiki/Woonsocket_City_Hall "Woonsocket City Hall"), and the conjoined [Honan's Block and 112-114 Main Street](/wiki/Honan%27s_Block_and_112-114_Main_Street "Honan's Block and 112-114 Main Street").[[2]](#cite_note-NRHP-2)
